news_items.headline: MLB End-of-May Check-in: AL West
news_items.summary: The Mariners sit atop the league’s most “meh” division.

news_items.headline: Astros News: Hader’s Return, Imai’s Turnaround, Abreu Struggles, Ullola
news_items.summary: The latest news on your Houston Astros! Josh Hader’s return appears to be imminent: After 6 no-hit innings in his best start in MLB, Tatsuya Imai gave the Astros another strong start this weekend against division-leading Milwaukee: Bryan Abreu’s struggles continue, so much so that the Astros have, at least for now, changed the trajectory […]

news_items.headline: Reds place Elly De La Cruz on the 10-day IL with a right hamstring strain
news_items.summary: Elly De La Cruz left a 6-4 victory over the Atlanta Braves with right hamstring tightness after singling to right center in the fifth inning.

news_items.headline: Reds series preview: Both teams hate the month of May
news_items.summary: Like the Royals, the Reds have been slumping.

news_items.headline: Dodgers in May: Injuries creep up, wins pile up
news_items.summary: The Dodgers’ depth took some hits during May, with a pair of injuries on both sides of the ball. But they withstood the losses and piled up the wins over the final three weeks of the month. After an incredibly stable starting rotation through the end of April, the Dodgers lost Tyler Glasnow to back […]

news_items.headline: The Giants’ top 10 trade chips
news_items.summary: The Giants have been racking up the Ls, but they might be able to run up the Ws at this year’s MLB trade deadline.

news_items.headline: Tracking offseason roster movement for Arizona baseball
news_items.summary: Instead of playing into June, as had been the case the previous five years, Arizona found itself done by mid-May after a disastrous 2026 season. It was a major regression from the year before, when the Wildcats returned to the College World Series for the 19th time. But if there’s an upside to such a […]
